    <h1 {...{
      "id": "filesystem-operations"
    }}>{`Filesystem Operations`}</h1>
    <p>{`UXP provides powerful APIs for reading, writing, creating, and deleting files. This guide will walk you through the concepts, permissions, and practical examples you need to work with the file system in your plugins`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "system-requirements"
    }}>{`System requirements`}</h2>
    <p>{`Please make make sure your development environment uses the following `}<strong parentName="p">{`minimum versions`}</strong>{` to avoid compatibility issues:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest v5`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "understand-file-system-access"
    }}>{`Understand File System Access`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-sandbox-model"
    }}>{`The Sandbox Model`}</h3>
    <p>{`UXP uses a security model that restricts file system access by default. This protected area is called the `}<strong parentName="p">{`sandbox`}</strong>{`—a set of safe locations your plugin can access without additional permissions.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`In plugins`}</strong>{`, the sandbox includes:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Plugin folder`}</strong>{`: Your plugin's installation directory (read-only)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Data folder`}</strong>{`: Persistent storage for your plugin's data`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Temporary folder`}</strong>{`: Transitory storage that may be cleared automatically`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`The Temporary folder is `}<em parentName="p">{`transitory`}</em>{` and may be cleared automatically; the Data folder is `}<em parentName="p">{`persistent`}</em>{` across app upgrades, but will be removed on uninstall or if the user clears it. Always `}<strong parentName="p">{`provide users with options to back up critical data`}</strong>{` to locations of their choice.`}</p>
    <p>{`While the sandbox is sufficient for many use cases, you may need to access other locations—such as the user's Documents folder or a specific project directory. UXP supports this through a `}<strong parentName="p">{`permission-based system`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "file-system-permissions"
    }}>{`File System Permissions`}</h3>
    <p>{`To access the file system, you must declare the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` permission in your plugin's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Before you proceed`}</p>
    <p>{`Make sure you understand how `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
      }}>{`Manifest permissions`}</a>{` work before implementing file operations in your plugin.`}</p>
    <p>{`The detailed `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`PermissionsDefinition`}</inlineCode>{` reference`}</a>{` explains all available permission options and their security implications.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` permission accepts three values:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode></strong>{` (default): provides access only to the sandbox locations. Your plugin will always be able to access the installation directory, data folder, and temporary folder.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`"request"`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: allows your plugin to request user permission via file picker dialogs`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: grants unrestricted access to the user's file system`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Here's how to declare this permission in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "plugin"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Best practices for permissions`}</p>
    <p>{`Choose the `}<em parentName="p">{`most accurate`}</em>{` permission level for your use case. In the future, users will be asked to provide explicit consent based on the permissions you request.`}</p>
    <p>{`While `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{` might seem like the easiest option, users may be uncomfortable granting full file system access unless it's absolutely necessary—and they might deny installation of your plugin altogether. Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{` when you need access to user-specified files, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{` (or simply omit the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` field, which defaults to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{`) when sandbox access is sufficient.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "url-schemes"
    }}>{`URL Schemes`}</h3>
    <p>{`UXP provides convenient URL schemes as shortcuts to specific file system locations:`}</p>

      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{

          <th parentName="tr" {...{

        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">

          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`plugin:/`}</inlineCode></td>

          <td parentName="tr" {...{

        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{

          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`plugin-data:/`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{

        <tr parentName="tbody">

          }}>{`Plugin temporary folder (read-write)`}</td>

        <tr parentName="tbody">

    <p>{`You can use these schemes in both HTML and JavaScript:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<img src="plugin:/icons/logo.png" />
<img src="file:/Users/user/Downloads/sample.png" /> <!-- update the path based on your system -->
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const dataFile = await localFileSystem.getEntryWithUrl("plugin-data:/settings.json");
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "choosing-the-right-api"
    }}>{`Choosing the Right API`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP provides two APIs for file system operations, each suited to different use cases:`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`API`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{

          }}>{`Best For`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{

      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">

        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-localfilesystem-api"
    }}>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="h3">{`LocalFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` API`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LocalFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` API is object-oriented and works with `}<strong parentName="p">{`Entry references`}</strong>{`—objects that represent files and folders. This approach is ideal when you need to perform multiple operations on the same file or traverse directory structures.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Access the API:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem, types } = require('uxp').storage;
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "understanding-entries"
    }}>{`Understanding Entries`}</h4>
    <p>{`The file system contains two types of items: files and folders. UXP represents these with the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`File`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Folder`}</inlineCode>{` classes, both of which extend a base class called `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Entry`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`Some methods return an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Entry`}</inlineCode>{` because the type can only be determined at runtime. You should check the entry type before performing type-specific operations:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem, types } = require('uxp').storage;

async function createFileInFolder() {
    try {
        // Create a folder entry
        const folderEntry = await localFileSystem.createEntryWithUrl(
            "plugin-temp:/myFolder",
            { type: types.folder }
        );

        // Verify it's a folder before using folder-specific methods
        if (folderEntry.isFolder) {
            const newFile = await folderEntry.createFile("data.txt", { overwrite: true });
            await newFile.write("This is sample content.");
            console.log(\`File created at: \${newFile.nativePath}\`);
        }
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to create file:", e);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-fs-module"
    }}>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="h3">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module provides a path-based API similar to `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://nodejs.org/api/fs.html"
      }}>{`Node.js file system operations`}</a>{`. It's ideal for straightforward, single-operation tasks like reading a configuration file or writing output.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Access the API:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const fs = require("fs");
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Now let's explore practical examples for different permission levels.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-localfilesystem"
    }}>{`Working with LocalFileSystem`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-1-accessing-the-sandbox"
    }}>{`Example 1: Accessing the Sandbox`}</h3>
    <p>{`When your plugin only needs to work with files in its own sandbox, use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{` permission level—or leave the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` field empty, which defaults to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{` anyway.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem } = require('uxp').storage;

async function accessPluginDataFolder() {
    // Access the plugin's data folder
    try {
        const dataFolder = await localFileSystem.getEntryWithUrl("plugin-data:/");
        console.log(\`Data folder path: \${dataFolder.nativePath}\`);

        // List all files in the data folder
        const entries = await dataFolder.getEntries();
        console.log(\`Found \${entries.length} items in data folder\`);

        for (const entry of entries) {
            console.log(\`- \${entry.name} (\${entry.isFile ? 'file' : 'folder'})\`);
        }
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to access data folder:", e);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "plugin"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-2-accessing-arbitrary-locations"
    }}>{`Example 2: Accessing Arbitrary Locations`}</h3>
    <p>{`When your plugin needs unrestricted access to the file system, use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{` permission level. This requires you to specify absolute file paths using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`file:/`}</inlineCode>{` scheme.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem } = require('uxp').storage;

async function accessUserDocuments() {
  // Access a specific location outside the sandbox
  try {
    // For macOS
    const documentsFolder = await localFileSystem.getEntryWithUrl(
      "file:/Users/user/Documents" // 👈 update with your user
    );
    // For Windows, use: "file:/C:/Users/user/Documents"

    console.log(\`Documents folder path: \${documentsFolder.nativePath}\`);

    // Read a specific file
    const configFile = await localFileSystem.getEntryWithUrl(
      "file:/Users/user/Documents/config.json" // 👈 update with your user
    );
    if (configFile.isFile) {
      const content = await configFile.read();
      console.log("Config file content:", content);
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Failed to access documents folder:", e);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-1"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "fullAccess"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Remember that users may be hesitant to install plugins requesting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{`. Only use this permission level when absolutely necessary. Consider using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{` instead to let users choose specific files or folders.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-3-user-selected-locations"
    }}>{`Example 3: User-Selected Locations`}</h3>
    <p>{`The most user-friendly approach is to let users choose which files or folders your plugin can access. Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{` permission level with file picker dialogs.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-2"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem, domains, fileTypes } = require('uxp').storage;

async function openUserSelectedFile() {
    // Present a file picker starting at the user's Desktop
    try {
        const file = await localFileSystem.getFileForOpening({
            initialDomain: domains.userDesktop,
            types: fileTypes.text
        });

        if (!file) {
            console.log("User cancelled file selection");
            return;
        }

        // Read the selected file
        const content = await file.read();
        console.log(\`File content:\\n\${content}\`);
        console.log(\`File path: \${file.nativePath}\`);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to open file:", err);
    }
}

async function saveToUserSelectedLocation() {
    // Present a save dialog to let the user choose where to save
    try {
        const file = await localFileSystem.getFileForSaving("export.txt", {
            types: ["txt"]
        });

        if (!file) {
            console.log("User cancelled save operation");
            return;
        }

        // Write content to the selected location
        await file.write("This content was exported from the plugin.");
        console.log(\`File saved to: \${file.nativePath}\`);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to save file:", err);
    }
}

async function selectFolderForExport() {
    // Let the user select a folder for batch export
    try {
        const folder = await localFileSystem.getFolder({
            initialDomain: domains.userDocuments
        });

        if (!folder) {
            console.log("User cancelled folder selection");
            return;
        }

        // Create multiple files in the selected folder
        for (let i = 1; i <= 3; i++) {
            const file = await folder.createFile(\`export_\${i}.txt\`, { overwrite: true });
            await file.write(\`Content for file \${i}\`);
        }

        console.log(\`Created 3 files in: \${folder.nativePath}\`);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to export files:", err);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{

  "manifestVersion": 5,

  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "request"

}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header,text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "domain-tokens"
    }}>{`Domain tokens`}</h4>
    <p>{`In the example above, we use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`domains.userDesktop`}</inlineCode>{` token to get the user's Desktop folder. You can use other domains tokens, such as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`domains.userDocuments`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`domains.userDownloads`}</inlineCode>{`, etc. Check the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/domains.md"
      }}>{`Domain Tokens`}</a>{` reference for the full list.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-4-remembering-user-selections-with-tokens"
    }}>{`Example 4: Remembering User Selections with Tokens`}</h3>
    <p>{`When users grant access to files or folders, you can create tokens to remember those locations across sessions—saving users from repeatedly selecting the same files.`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP provides two types of tokens:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/FileSystemProvider.md"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Session tokens`}</strong></a>{`: Last until the plugin is unloaded or the application closes`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/FileSystemProvider.md"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Persistent tokens`}</strong></a>{`: Survive across sessions until the plugin is uninstalled`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { localFileSystem, domains, fileTypes } = require('uxp').storage;

async function selectAndRememberFile() {
    try {
        // Let the user select a file
        const file = await localFileSystem.getFileForOpening({
            initialDomain: domains.userDesktop,
            types: fileTypes.text
        });

        if (!file) {
            console.log("User cancelled file selection");
            return;
        }

        // Create a persistent token for this file
        const token = await localFileSystem.createPersistentToken(file);

        // Store the token for future use (e.g., in localStorage)
        localStorage.setItem("selectedFileToken", token);

        console.log(\`File selected and token saved: \${file.nativePath}\`);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to create token:", err);
    }
}

async function readPreviouslySelectedFile() {
    try {
        // Retrieve the token from storage
        const token = localStorage.getItem("selectedFileToken");

        if (!token) {
            console.log("No previously selected file found");
            return;
        }

        // Access the file using the token
        const file = await localFileSystem.getEntryForPersistentToken(token);

        // Read the file content
        const content = await file.read();
        console.log(\`File content:\\n\${content}\`);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to read file using token:", err);
        // Token may be invalid if file was deleted or moved
        localStorage.removeItem("selectedFileToken");
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Token Storage`}</p>
    <p>{`Store tokens in `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/Data%20Storage/LocalStorage.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`localStorage`}</inlineCode></a>{` or your plugin's data folder so they persist between plugin sessions. Always handle cases where tokens become invalid (e.g., if the user deletes or moves the file).`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "localfilesystem-api-reference"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h3">{`LocalFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` API Reference`}</h3>
    <p>{`For complete API documentation, see:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/FileSystemProvider.md"
        }}>{`FileSystemProvider`}</a>{`: main interface for file system operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/Entry.md"
        }}>{`Entry`}</a>{`: base class for files and folders.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/File.md"
        }}>{`File`}</a>{`: file-specific operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/Folder.md"
        }}>{`Folder`}</a>{`: folder-specific operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/Persistent%20File%20Storage/EntryMetadata.md"
        }}>{`EntryMetadata`}</a>{`: file and folder metadata.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/Path.md"
        }}>{`Path`}</a>{`: path manipulation utilities.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-the-fs-module"
    }}>{`Working with the `}<inlineCode parentName="h2">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module offers a simpler, path-based approach similar to `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://nodejs.org/api/fs.html"
      }}>{`Node.js`}</a>{`. It's ideal for quick, single-operation tasks.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-5-reading-from-the-sandbox"
    }}>{`Example 5: Reading from the Sandbox`}</h3>
    <p>{`Read a file directly from the sandbox using a URL scheme:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-3"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const fs = require("fs");

async function readConfigFile() {
    // Read a configuration file from the plugin folder
    try {
        const content = await fs.readFile("plugin:/config.json", "utf8");
        const config = JSON.parse(content);
        console.log("Configuration loaded:", config);
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to read config file:", e);
    }
}

async function writeToDataFolder() {
    // Write data to the plugin's data folder
    try {
        const data = {
            lastRun: new Date().toISOString(),
            version: "1.0.0"
        };

        await fs.writeFile(
            "plugin-data:/state.json",
            JSON.stringify(data, null, 2),
            "utf-8"
        );

        console.log("State saved successfully");
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to save state:", e);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-3"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "plugin"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`As shown in the example above, you can use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`plugin:/`}</inlineCode>{` URL scheme in both `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` APIs .`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-6-writing-to-arbitrary-locations"
    }}>{`Example 6: Writing to Arbitrary Locations`}</h3>
    <p>{`Write files to any location on the file system using absolute paths:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-4"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const fs = require("fs");

async function exportToDesktop() {
    // Export data to the user's Desktop
    try {
        const exportData = "Exported data from plugin";

        // For macOS
        await fs.writeFile(
            "/Users/user/Desktop/export.txt", // 👈 update with your user
            exportData,
            { encoding: "utf-8" }
        );
        // For Windows, use: "C:/Users/user/Desktop/export.txt"

        console.log("File exported to Desktop");
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to export file:", e);
    }
}

async function readFromDesktop() {
    // Read a file from the user's Desktop folder
    try {
        // For macOS
        const content = await fs.readFile(
            "/Users/user/Desktop/export.txt",  // 👈 update with your user
            "utf8"
        );
        // For Windows, use: "C:/Users/user/Desktop/export.txt"

        console.log("File content:", content);
    } catch (e) {
        console.error("Failed to read file:", e);
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-4"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "localFileSystem": "fullAccess"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "fs-module-api-reference"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h3">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module API Reference`}</h3>
    <p>{`For complete API documentation, see:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/fs/"
        }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`fs`}</inlineCode>{` module`}</a>{`: complete file system API reference.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/Path.md"
        }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`path`}</inlineCode></a>{`: path manipulation utilities.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "additional-considerations"
    }}>{`Additional Considerations`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "operating-system-limitations"
    }}>{`Operating System Limitations`}</h3>
    <p>{`Even with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{` permission, certain system locations may be restricted by the operating system:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`macOS and Windows`}</strong>{`: Generally allow access to most user-accessible locations`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UWP (Windows Store apps)`}</strong>{`: System folders are prohibited and cannot be accessed`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Always handle file system errors gracefully and inform users when access is denied. Operating system security policies may prevent access to certain locations even when your plugin has the correct permissions.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h3>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Choose the right permission level`}</strong>{`: start with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{`, upgrade to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{`, and only use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{` when absolutely necessary.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Handle errors gracefully`}</strong>{`: always wrap file operations in try-catch blocks.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Validate file paths`}</strong>{`: check that files exist before attempting to read them.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Use appropriate encoding`}</strong>{`: specify `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"utf-8"`}</inlineCode>{` for text files to ensure proper character handling.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Provide user feedback`}</strong>{`: show progress indicators for long-running file operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Clean up temporary files`}</strong>{`: delete temporary files when they're no longer needed.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-to-use-each-api"
    }}>{`When to Use Each API`}</h3>
    <table>

    <ul>

        }}>{`Local File System Permission`}</a>{`: detailed permission documentation.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`The Sandbox Model`}</strong>{`: By default, plugins can only access safe locations (plugin folder, data folder, and temporary folder) without additional permissions. The plugin folder is read-only, while data and temporary folders are read-write but may be cleared automatically.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`File System Permissions`}</strong>{`: Declare the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` permission in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` with three possible values:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"plugin"`}</inlineCode>{`: Access only sandbox locations (default, most secure)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{`: Present file picker dialogs for user-selected files and folders (recommended for user data)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{`: Unrestricted file system access (use sparingly, may deter users from installing)`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`URL Schemes`}</strong>{`: UXP provides convenient shortcuts to file system locations:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin:/`}</inlineCode>{` for the plugin folder (read-only)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin-data:/`}</inlineCode>{` for persistent plugin data`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin-temp:/`}</inlineCode>{` for temporary storage`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`file:/`}</inlineCode>{` for absolute paths (requires `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Two File System APIs`}</strong>{`: Choose based on your use case:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`LocalFileSystem API`}</strong>{`: Object-oriented, uses Entry references (File and Folder objects), ideal for multiple operations, directory traversal, and working with metadata`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`fs module`}</strong>{`: Path-based like Node.js, ideal for quick single operations and simple read/write tasks`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Persistent Access with Tokens`}</strong>{`: When users grant access to files or folders via `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{` permission, create session tokens (temporary) or persistent tokens (survive across sessions) to remember these locations without repeatedly prompting users.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Common Patterns`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`For configuration files in your plugin folder: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`fs.readFile("plugin:/config.json")`}</inlineCode></li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`For saving plugin state: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`fs.writeFile("plugin-data:/state.json")`}</inlineCode></li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`For user-selected files: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`localFileSystem.getFileForOpening()`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"request"`}</inlineCode>{` permission`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`For batch operations: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`LocalFileSystem`}</inlineCode>{` API with folder traversal`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`For remembering user selections: Create persistent tokens and store them in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`localStorage`}</inlineCode></li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
